items.title: Damning report finds WA prisoners subjected to 'cruel, degrading treatment'

children.content: WA prisoners are being subjected to "cruel, inhumane or degrading treatment" because the state government has allowed prisons to become "almost fundamentally broken", according to the Inspector of Custodial Services.

gallery.altText: A crowded cell with four beds.

thumbnail.altText: A crowded cell with four beds.

items.title: Notre Dame university halts new nursing student enrolments for another year

image.altText: A sign for the University of Notre Dame at its Fremantle campus

children.content: The university says it will not take new students for its nursing program for another year, as it needs to "rebalance enrolments across the program within accredited program capacity".

items.title: Former WA Police officer 'gutted' after compensation for horrific injury denied

children.content: Former WA Police officer and WA Australian of the Year Paul Litherland says he is "heartbroken" after his claim for post-service medical compensation over injuries he received in the force was denied.

image.altText: Profile shot of Paul Litherland

items.title: Residents shocked by severe storm as South West WA homes left flooded

children.content: An unexpected deluge has flooded homes and public facilities in the South West WA town of Harvey. The community was pelted with 40mm of rain in one hour.

image.altText: Flooding in a street in the town of Harvey

items.title: New leader, old problem for WA Liberals as Zempilas flirts with One Nation

children.content: As Basil Zempilas flirts with One Nation, will the right-wing populist party drag the WA Liberals down again, or will it become a life raft on the way to a political resurrection?

articles.title: Ignoring One Nation like 'ignoring will of people', Basil Zempilas warns

children.content: WA Opposition Leader Basil Zempilas says One Nation deserves "respect" and he is open to finding a way of working with the right-wing populist party as support for it grows.

image.altText: Basil Zempilas stands at a podium and addresses the crowd

gallery.altText: A close up of the torso of a man in a suit surrounded by people
A woman speaks to the camera
A man in a navy cap wears a bright orange shirt with the words  "We've got the guts to say what you're thinking" on the back.

A woman speaks to the camera

items.title: Incorrect Medicare billing allegations spark concerns of broader issue

children.content: One of Australia's largest medical imaging providers, Integral Diagnostics, says it will look into allegations of improper Medicare billing at a regional WA public hospital where the company provides radiology services.

image.altText: A hand holds a medicare card

items.title: From the Kimberley to Sweden, how heartbreak helped me take a travel leap

children.content: As I approached the age of 30, I longed to travel outside of Australia, but there was always an excuse or obstacle. A chance to share my culture with the world finally got me there.

image.altText: Molly in front of some of her art

items.title: Calls for kindness as community mourns death of shark attack victim

children.content: The Albany community is still coming to terms with the loss of resident Daniel Turpin after he did not survive a shark attack at the weekend.

articles.title: Daniel Turpin named as diver killed in WA shark attack

children.content: The family of a 35-year-old diver who died after being bitten by a shark has described him as "an adored husband, son, brother and uncle who had a lifelong love and deep respect for the ocean".

image.altText: A man with short brown hair and a white shirt.

gallery.altText: Compositie image of a man on left and marina on right.

thumbnail.altText: Compositie image of a man on left and marina on right.

items.title: Why a property downturn would be a blessing for the RBA

children.content: With two Australian banks cutting fixed home loan rates, there is a ray of hope that the cycle of interest rate hikes has peaked and borrowing costs may be lower down the track — another sign the pace of economic change has moved into overdrive.

image.altText: a skyline with houses

items.title: Year 12 students in Melbourne caught cheating using AI

children.content: An all-boys school in Melbourne's outer east has taken action after several students were caught cheating on a Year 12 exam using AI.

image.altText: Three students in dark school blazers, holding small boxes. Their faces are not in the picture.

items.title: 'Hidden cameras' captured Perth paediatrician's alleged sexual offending, court hears

children.content: A paediatrician charged with child sex offences may have up to nine victims, with alleged offending captured on "hidden cameras" in consulting rooms, a Perth court hears.

image.altText: A close up of a man wearing glasses and a suit

items.title: Doctors call for mandatory food star ratings to stop health 'crisis'

children.content: Health bodies want star ratings on all packaged foods to help shoppers make healthier choices, but the grocery industry says the voluntary system works.

image.altText: A logo with four stars and the caption Health Star Rating on a food packet.

items.title: Perth man wants compensation for forensic examination damage

children.content: Troy Coward's investment property was the scene of a brutal murder involving his tenants. More than 13 years later, he is fighting for compensation from police over the impact he says their investigation had on his health and finances.

gallery.altText: Man with short hair and dark brown blazer, arms folded, with trees and pathway in background
Troy Coward's East Perth apartment bathroom covered in smudgy black marks
The laundry area of Troy Coward's former apartment

Troy Coward's East Perth apartment bathroom covered in smudgy black marks

items.title: ABC boss confirms ex-news director in the dark about replacement hunt

children.content: Managing director Hugh Marks is questioned by Sally Sara over Justin Stevens's sudden resignation last month, in addition to controversies over the hiring of Grace Tame and subsequent comments by radio host Charlie Pickering.

image.altText: An older man in a suit stares off to the side

items.title: Six of the most contentious parts of the NDIS bill

children.content: Politicians are presiding over three days of public hearings into a bill paving the way for the biggest ever cuts to the NDIS.

image.altText: A graphic illustration of a person in a wheelchair, an NDIS logo and a pair of scissors

items.title: Obscene acts charges prompt calls for more CCTV

children.content: A woman who reported a terrifying incident while walking at night in Derby says more CCTV cameras would increase safety and help monitor popular locations.

A CCTV camera on a wall near an exit sign
Maria Sandoval takes a selfie with the beach in the background.

items.title: Idris Elba thinks audiences won't support a black James Bond

children.content: Idris Elba has quashed rumours he was ever in the running to play James Bond, saying audiences are unlikely to come along for the ride if Bond is played by a black actor.

image.altText: Idris Elba in a boat on a body of water, waving, wearing a dark t-shirt

items.title: WA opposition set to introduce stronger train lighting legislation

children.content: Advocates welcome a WA opposition bill enforcing better train lighting, after decades of campaigning.

image.altText: Three women holding bouquets of flowers walk to a place near a paddock to put the flowers.

items.title: Rare bird sighting excites twitchers

children.content: Birdwatchers from across Perth and even interstate have flocked to WA's Midwest after the sighting of a black-headed gull.

gallery.altText: a silver grey gull with black head wings spread in flight over a beach

Man crawls commando style on beach with large camera and gulls

items.title: Matildas look for redemption against Mexico in Foord's 150th

children.content: The Matildas will be hoping for a degree of redemption as they look to put Saturday night's disjointed performance in Newcastle to bed in their second international against Mexico. Follow live.

image.altText: Caitlin Foord runs with the ball

items.title: Healthy people in hospitals are costing millions every day

children.content: As many medically well patients wait in public hospitals for a spot in aged care, their bodies begin to deteriorate at a rate experts believe is preventable.

image.altText: A bed in a resident's room in an aged care home.

items.title: The criticism Marc Fennell always gets for Stuff the British Stole

children.content: Two years in the making, the Walkley Award-winning journalist is back with the third season of his globe-trotting historical mystery series. He's treating it like it's the last.

image.altText: Marc looks to the left while standing around lush greenery.

items.title: Mechelle Turvey among 'outstanding' Australians on King's Birthday Honours roll

children.content: Almost 950 Australians are honoured, including five stand-out recipients who are now Companions of the Order — among them, the mother of murdered Noongar-Yamatji schoolboy Cassius Turvey.

image.altText: A graphic with the images of three women and two men.

items.title: Four-time veterans to late bolters, here's your Socceroos pocket guide

children.content: Mat Ryan and Mathew Leckie are at a record-equalling fourth FIFA World Cup, while Cristian Volpato headlines a host of first-timers, after his late switch from Italy. Here's your complete guide to the Socceroos' World Cup squad.

articles.title: Somali referee denied entry to US days out from start of World Cup

children.content: Highly rated Somali referee Omar Artan is turned away from the US just days before he was expected to officiate in the FIFA World Cup.

image.altText: Omar Artan, wearing a blue referree uniform, looks to his left.

articles.title: Eriksen 'doing well' after on-field collapse during Denmark game

children.content: Soccer star Christian Eriksen says he is at home with family and "doing well" after collapsing on the field again while playing for Denmark.

image.altText: Two soccer players on a field, one in red and white and one in yellow and blue

articles.title: Will you be in North America for the FIFA World Cup? We want to hear from you

children.content: If you're an Aussie fan making the trip, or already there, share your experience with us.
